author: Miriam Polzer
message_subject: Add sigma types to precomputed points
message_body:
I've also added some more translation functions, mostly to practice the
tactics.

author: Miriam Polzer
message_subject: Ed25519: Point-based separation logic for doubling
message_body:
Convert the doubling function to a point based format, the rest of the
functions will follow.

Add a tactic for deallocation, to be used in other proofs too.

author: Alix Trieu
message_subject: Bedrock2 secp256k1 point operations

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Factor out some logic from UnsaturatedSolinas.to_bytes_correct (#1449)

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Split off the non-bedrock-dependent part of equiv proofs
message_body:
This will hopefully make development a bit faster; these files are so
long.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: More WIP on asm equiv (#1124)

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Update asm equiv to finish all but output goal

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Improve merge{,_node} automation in symex_ident_correct

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Massively speed up symex_ident_correct and improve warning messages
message_body:
`inversion` on `ErrorT` types was taking forever, so now we use a
dedicated tactic.

Additionally, some goals that were previously proven by my automation no
longer are, so I've added some warning messages marking these cases.  In
particular, the automation does not currently handle nested
`merge`/`merge_node` calls in a single branch, because there's no clear
way to determine automatically what the subcalls should evaluate to.
Perhaps the automation should be tweaked to leave over evars for unknown
evaluations?

author: jadep
message_subject: Copy over ECC content from rupicola

author: jadep
message_subject: clean up/automate UnsaturatedSolinas interface

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add a Go backend

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Use a slightly different strategy for printing ident inductives
message_body:
We now first generate the type of the eliminator, and then have a single
function responsible for printing.  This should hopefully make it easier
to convert to OCaml.

We also now emit all inductives in the same file.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Integrate Wf and Interp proofs
message_body:
Now the only admits remaining in Toplevel1 are glue ones about
freeze/to_bytes/from_bytes/fe_nonzero.  What remains (beyond those
admits, and the ones about word-by-word montgomery building blocks in
Arithmetic), are the proofs about abstract interpretation and the
rewriter.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Reorganize the curve-specific synthesis framework
message_body:
This brings in most of the changes that I made when figuring out how to
integrate montgomery into the framework.

The code is a bit slower because the we drop `Print Assumptions` at the
bottom of each synthesis problem, to record that things are closed under
the global context.  If we remove this, we get back the time that we
lost with this commit.

message_subject: Add missing file

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Factor out some of the preglue synthesis code
message_body:
This makes it a bit more uniform, and hopefully more automatable and
packageable.  Unfortunately, there's still no spec for this part of the
pipeline, so the tactics simply aggregate common patterns.

Alas, this also makes things a bit slower; I suspect that [Defined] is
the place where things are slower.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Don't rely on autogenerated names
message_body:
This fixes all of the private-names warnings emitted by
compiling fiat-crypto with https://github.com/coq/coq/pull/268 (minus
the ones in coqprime, which I didn't touch).
